definition.title: The Real Housewives of Trucking
definition.objective: Penetrate female-skewed trucker-wife/partner segment. Build brand warmth before commercial offers. Brand repositioning from driver-facing operational to family-aware community brand. Expand reach into driver spouse demographic through humor and relatable storytelling. Create raving fans among spouses who influence driver decisions. Funnel: Outriders Club (spouse as advocate → driver membership).
definition.target_audience: Trucker wives and partners (primary), women in trucking households, female audiences within trucking culture. Secondary: male drivers who share with partners, trucking families. Funnel target: Outriders Club membership via spouse advocacy.
definition.cadence: Ep 1 LIVE on YouTube. Anj distributing to trucker wife FB communities + female-skewed paid targeting. $20/day targeted ad spend baseline. Track engagement for Ep 2/3 greenlight. Weekly episodic series planned (12-15 episodes).

top_level_summary: The trucking industry is overwhelmingly male-dominated; however, in many households the spouse — most often the wife — plays a significant role in decision-making regarding a driver's work environment, job satisfaction, and long-term career choices. For many drivers, their spouse is the primary advisor on issues such as safety, parking conditions, company reputation, and overall quality of life on the road. The Real Housewives of Trucking campaign is designed to strategically reach this influential audience and position LineHaul Station and the Outriders Club as a brand that understands and supports both the driver and the family behind them. This initiative is not intended to replace our core driver-focused marketing, but rather to serve as a supplemental campaign that expands our reach into a demographic that is often overlooked in trucking marketing: the driver's spouse. By engaging trucker wives and partners through humor and relatable storytelling, the campaign aims to increase brand awareness among trucking families, encourage spouses to advocate for their drivers to join the Outriders Club, build goodwill toward LineHaul Station as a driver-first community, and expand engagement among female audiences within trucking culture. Ultimately, the goal is to create raving fans of LineHaul Station among the spouses of drivers, who in turn influence the driver's decision to engage with our community. The campaign will utilize a modest but highly targeted ad spend focusing on demographics and communities where trucking spouses are already active online, with a recommended baseline of $20/day targeted advertising. While dedicated trucker wives groups exist with approximately 8,000 total members, analysis of major trucking influencer pages (approximately 100 high-traffic pages) reveals that spouses frequently engage in comments and reactions, often demonstrating stronger and more positive engagement than drivers themselves. The initial Real Housewives of Trucking video has demonstrated strong engagement and humor resonance with an internal grade of A / 9.4 out of 10. The next phase will expand into a short-form episodic series of 12-15 episodes with recurring characters, themes focused on helping drivers and supporting one another. Unlike traditional reality shows that thrive on conflict, Real Housewives of Trucking leans into a positive comedic twist: instead of rivalry and backstabbing, these characters are united by a common goal — making sure their drivers have the best possible experience on the road. Most trucking marketing focuses exclusively on drivers. By engaging the spouse audience, LineHaul Station can tap into a powerful but largely ignored influence channel within trucking families. This campaign positions LineHaul Station not just as a service for drivers, but as a community that understands and supports the entire trucking household.

asset_readiness.summary: This campaign has READY assets for calendar deployment. Ep 1 is LIVE on YouTube and available for clipping, reposting, and social distribution. 4 static character ads (Bethany + Big Dumper Jones) are AVAILABLE. Additional assets (episodic series, landing page, apparel) are in development.

assets.asset: YouTube Episode 1 — Real Housewives of Trucking
assets.description: Full episode LIVE on YouTube. Comedy spoof — the show is failing because trucker wives are too nice. Internal grade: A / 9.4 out of 10. IP built, universe scalable. Available for clipping into short-form reels, social posts, story cuts, and paid ad creative.

assets.asset: Static Character Ads — Bethany + Big Dumper Jones (4 statics)
assets.description: 4 static ad images featuring campaign characters Bethany and Big Dumper Jones. Ready for Facebook, Instagram, and paid targeting deployment.

Concept: use ChatGPT/AI to generate images of models wearing Real Housewives of Trucking shirts. Creates static ad content without producing physical merchandise. Front: subtle logo. Back: big funny statement. Target aesthetic: cute enough that women say 'I gotta have that.'
assets.blocker: Mockups not yet generated. Shirt designs not yet finalized.

assets.description: Weekly episodic series, 12-15 episodes, 30-60 second short-form videos. Recurring characters from Ep 1. Themes: helping drivers, supporting one another. Positive comedic twist (no rivalry/backstabbing). Concepts include: 'The Parking Situation,' 'The Stress Test,' 'Truck Stop Intelligence Network,' 'Driver Evaluation,' 'The Real Boss,' 'Trucker Wife Confessionals' (Bravo-style).
assets.blocker: Scripts not yet written. Episodes not yet produced.

assets.asset: Spouse Interest / Fan Club Landing Page
assets.description: Landing page for spouse engagement. NOT a membership form — a fan club / insider list. Headline: 'Real Housewives of Trucking.' Low-friction form: Your Name, Your Email, optional Driver's Name + Driver's Email. CTA: 'Send My Driver The Invite.' Two auto-emails fire: (1) welcome to wife with YouTube ep + merch, (2) email to driver: 'Your spouse thinks you should see this.' Structure follows EO/YPO model: driver is the dues-paying member, spouse is connected participant with event access.
assets.blocker: Landing page not yet built. Email sequences not yet written.

assets.asset: Real Housewives of Trucking Apparel Line
assets.description: Apparel concepts for micro-commerce via on-demand printing (Tom's Sport — print one at a time). Designs: 'Real Housewives of Trucking,' 'Driver Support Crew,' 'Logistics Queen,' 'Professional Trucker Wife.' Style: subtle logo front, big statement back. LineHaul Station branding maintained. Must feel cute/funny/proud — not corporate.
assets.blocker: Designs not finalized. Printing pipeline not activated.

assets.description: Campaign memes, still images, and social graphics featuring Real Housewives of Trucking characters and humor. For organic posting and paid distribution.

Local spousal/couples events at LineHaul Station locations. Model: EO/YPO — member-only meetings plus couples events plus spouse-only events. Example: Outriders Memphis Spousal Meetup. Spouse can attend even when driver is on the road. Builds loyalty, referrals, social proof.
assets.blocker: Concept only — no events scheduled, no location open yet

next_actions: Continue Ep 1 distribution to trucker wife FB communities
Deploy 4 Bethany/Big Dumper static ads with $20/day targeted spend
Clip Ep 1 into short-form reels for Instagram and Facebook
Build spouse interest/fan club landing page (NOT membership — insider list)
Write email sequences: wife welcome + driver invite trigger
Generate AI apparel mockup statics (shirts on models via ChatGPT)
Finalize Real Housewives of Trucking shirt designs for Tom's Sport on-demand printing
Script Ep 2 and Ep 3 based on Ep 1 engagement data
Create meme statics and social graphics from Ep 1 characters
Track engagement metrics: reaction types (~30% laugh target), shares, spouse vs driver engagement
Set up LeadEnforce targeting on trucking influencer page followers

campaign_card.target_audience: Trucker wives and partners (primary), women in trucking households, female audiences within trucking culture. Secondary: male drivers who share with partners. Funnel: Outriders Club membership via spouse advocacy.
key_messages: Behind every good trucker is someone making sure they're treated right — and that's who we're marketing to.
The video is the spark, the funnel is the fire. Apparel is tribe, tribe is referral, referral is members.
No trucking brand markets to spouses. But spouses influence job changes, companies drivers stay with, safety choices, and retirement.

cta.text: Join the Housewives Insider List / Send My Driver The Invite
cta.lead_capture_point: Real Housewives of Trucking landing page → spouse interest form → driver invite email → Outriders Club

_legacy_card.description: EP 1 LIVE on YouTube. Internal grade: A / 9.4 out of 10. IP built. Universe scalable. Audience affinity first, conversion second. The show's premise: it's failing because trucker wives are too nice. The real emotional hook: truckers' spouses are the real operations managers of the trucking family. They care about where their driver sleeps, safety, stress levels, time at home. No trucking brand markets to spouses — but spouses influence job changes, companies drivers stay with, safety choices, and retirement. This campaign taps the real decision maker. Outriders Club structure: driver is the dues-paying member (must hold CDL), spouse is connected participant. Model follows EO/YPO — member-only meetings, couples events, spouse-only events. Spouses do not get a separate member number or profile — they are linked to the member in the database. Funnel: spouse sees content → joins insider list (low-friction: name + email, optional driver info) → driver gets invite email → driver signs membership agreement → both are enrolled. 4 static character ads available (Bethany + Big Dumper Jones). $20/day targeted ad spend recommended. Initial data shows ~30% laugh reaction rate on Facebook — comedic approach resonates. Approximately 8,000 members across dedicated trucker wife FB groups, but major trucking influencer pages (~100 high-traffic) show strong spouse engagement in comments. LeadEnforce can target followers of these influencer pages.
_legacy_card.strategic_purpose: Penetrate female-skewed trucker-wife/partner segment. Build brand warmth before commercial offers. Brand repositioning from driver-facing operational to family-aware community brand. Create raving fans among spouses who influence driver membership decisions. Funnel: Outriders Club. This campaign positions LineHaul Station not just as a service for drivers, but as a community that understands and supports the entire trucking household.
_legacy_card.asset_links:
VIDEOS:
- YouTube Ep 1 (LIVE — READY): https://youtu.be/fGngFyEBkcU?si=7OX1UfilhgzUnJJP — Full comedy spoof episode. Available for clipping into short-form reels, social posts, story cuts, and paid ad creative.
- Episodic Series Ep 2-15 (PLANNED — NOT READY): 12-15 episodes, 30-60 sec short-form. Concepts: 'The Parking Situation' (wives comparing where drivers sleep), 'The Stress Test' (happy driver vs stressed driver), 'Truck Stop Intelligence Network' (wives group chat), 'Driver Evaluation' (wife interviews husband), 'The Real Boss' (driver thinks he decides, wife holds phone), 'Trucker Wife Confessionals' (Bravo-style interview clips).

IMAGES / STATICS:
- 4 Static Character Ads: Bethany + Big Dumper Jones (AVAILABLE — READY). Ready for Facebook, Instagram, and paid targeting.
- AI Apparel Mockup Statics (CONCEPT — NOT READY): ChatGPT-generated images of models wearing RHOT shirts. Creates ad content without producing physical merch.
- Meme Statics and Social Graphics (PLANNED — NOT READY): Campaign memes and character-based humor graphics.

WEB / LANDING PAGE:
- Spouse Interest / Fan Club Landing Page (PLANNED — NOT READY): Low-friction insider list. Form: Your Name, Email, optional Driver Name + Email. CTA: 'Send My Driver The Invite.' Two auto-emails: (1) wife welcome with YouTube ep + merch, (2) driver invite: 'Your spouse thinks you should see this.' NOT a membership form — a fan club.

MERCHANDISE:
- Real Housewives of Trucking Apparel (CONCEPT — NOT READY): On-demand printing via Tom's Sport (single-shirt production). Designs: 'Real Housewives of Trucking,' 'Driver Support Crew,' 'Logistics Queen,' 'Professional Trucker Wife.' Subtle logo front, big statement back. LineHaul Station branded. Must feel cute/funny/proud. Also: 'Outrider Wife,' 'Sleeps Better When My Driver Has Parking.'

EVENTS:
- Outriders Spousal Events (CONCEPT — NOT READY): Local events at LHS locations. Spouse can attend when driver is on the road. Builds loyalty, referrals, social proof. Example: Outriders Memphis Spousal Meetup.

ADVERTISING:
- $20/day targeted spend on Facebook + Instagram. Target: trucking influencer page followers via LeadEnforce. Initial data: ~30% laugh reaction rate. Trucking wife FB groups (~8,000 members) + influencer pages (~100 high-traffic, strong spouse engagement in comments).
_legacy_card.pre_approved_angles: The wives are the heroes. The producers are the clowns. Meme-first. Affinity-first. Conversion second. Apparel is tribe. Tribe is referral. Referral is members. Behind every good trucker is someone making sure they're treated right. No trucking brand markets to spouses — but spouses influence everything. Trucker wives as logistics managers / operations managers of the family. Happy driver = happy house. Spouses as the real decision makers. The show fails because trucker wives are too nice. Outriders Club is for both — driver AND spouse. EO/YPO model. Cute/funny apparel that women want to wear.
_legacy_card.artlist_cost: Ep 1 fully produced (zero additional cost). Apparel mockups via AI generation (zero cost until print commitment). $20/day ad spend ($140/week). LeadEnforce targeting setup. Tom's Sport on-demand printing (per-unit cost on orders).
_legacy_card.repurposing: Ep 1 → short-form reels (30-60 sec clips), Instagram stories, Facebook video posts, paid ad creative cuts. Character statics → Facebook/Instagram ads, organic posts, meme formats. Apparel mockups → static ads, Instagram posts, story content. Episode concepts → social teasers, anticipation posts. Wife confessionals → standalone social clips. Landing page → organic sharing, email forwarding. Merch photos → aspirational lifestyle posts.
_legacy_card.timeline: Ep 1 LIVE and distributing. 4 static character ads AVAILABLE. $20/day ad targeting ACTIVE. Landing page PLANNED. Apparel mockups CONCEPT. Episodic series Ep 2-15 PLANNED — greenlight based on Ep 1 engagement data. Meme statics PLANNED. LeadEnforce setup PENDING.
_legacy_card.language_rules: Campaign tone must stay fun, light, and playful — NOT corporate. If it turns into forms, membership talk, and rules, it will die. Keep the insider joke energy. 'Real Housewives of Trucking' is a parody — lean into the humor. Spouse engagement should feel like joining a fan club, not filling out paperwork. LineHaul Station branding must remain visible on all assets but should not overwhelm the comedic tone.
_legacy_card.funnel_structure: Outriders Club membership model (EO/YPO structure): Driver = dues-paying member (CDL required). Spouse = connected participant (no separate member number, no profile, linked to member in database). Spouse captured via interest form (name + email + optional driver info). Driver receives invite email from spouse action. Driver completes full membership agreement separately. Spousal events: member-only meetings, couples events, spouse-only events. Exception: honorary memberships for industry people without CDL (special invites only).
